mnemonics: M.I.S.S.I.N.G D.A.T.A: Methods Involved Systematically Scrutinize Incomplete Numbers, Generating Decisions About Treatment Approaches
keyPoints: Identify missing data patterns (MCAR, MAR, MNAR) to choose the right handling method.
Use deletion (listwise/pairwise) for minimal, random missingness; use imputation (mean/median/mode) for larger, systematic gaps.
Advanced imputation (regression, MICE) predicts missing values based on relationships with other variables for more accuracy.
commonMistakes: Deleting data with a missingness pattern (e.g., MNAR) without consideration, introducing severe bias into the analysis.
Using mean imputation for a skewed distribution, which misrepresents the data's central tendency and variance.
Failing to report the methods and amount of missing data handled, making the analysis irreproducible.
